tables.name: persons
tables.description: Pipedrive-style person candidates from Salesforce Contact. Join account on AccountId for organization name when needed.

columns.name: sf_contact_id
columns.description: Salesforce Contact Id (preserve for traceability and idempotent load later).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full display name — trim; combine FirstName and LastName with a single space.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Primary email; lowercased and trimmed (use Email if present).

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Preferred phone — Phone or MobilePhone.

columns.name: org_name
columns.description: Account name looked up via Contact.AccountId = Account.Id (staging table account).

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map from OwnerId if you can coerce to integer, otherwise null.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Contact.CreatedDate if available.

tables.name: organizations
tables.description: Pipedrive-style organization candidates from Salesforce Account.

columns.name: sf_account_id
columns.description: Salesforce Account Id (preserve for traceability).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Account Name; trimmed.

columns.name: address
columns.description: Billing address summary — concatenate BillingStreet, BillingCity, BillingState, BillingPostalCode, BillingCountry with commas/spaces.

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map OwnerId when possible.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Account.CreatedDate if present.

column_notes.explanation: Directly maps the Salesforce Contact ID, cast to text for traceability.
Combines first and last names with a single space, handling nulls with empty strings, then trims the result.
Trims whitespace and lowercases the primary email field.
Prioritizes the Phone number, falling back to MobilePhone if the former is missing.
Retrieves the organization name by joining Contact and Account tables on the AccountId.
Uses regex matching to ensure OwnerId is purely numeric before coercing it to an integer; otherwise returns NULL.
Maps the Salesforce contact creation timestamp.

tables.reasoning: This model joins the Contact and Account source tables using a LEFT JOIN on AccountId to retrieve the organization name. It handles string normalization (trimming/lowercasing) for names and emails, uses COALESCE to select the preferred phone number, and employs a regular expression check to safely coerce OwnerIds to integers only when they consist solely of digits, preventing casting errors on Salesforce-prefixed IDs.

column_notes.explanation: Trimmed and cast to text for traceability.
Trimmed account name converted to display format.
Concatenated billing fields with commas, handling NULLs and empty strings safely.
Attempted coercion to integer; returns NULL if source contains non-numeric Salesforce IDs (regex check).
Created date from account, cast to timestamp.

tables.reasoning: The model maps the `account` table to the `organizations` schema. The address is safely concatenated using conditional checks for NULLs and empty strings to avoid stray commas. The `owner_id` uses a regex check (`~ '^[0-9]+$'`) to handle the "coerce when possible" requirement for integer conversion, returning NULL for valid but non-numeric Salesforce IDs (e.g., '005...').

column_notes.explanation: Salesforce Contact Id, preserved directly for traceability and idempotent loads.
Combined FirstName and LastName with CONCAT_WS using a single space, then TRIMMED to clean whitespace.
Email address, lowercased and trimmed for consistency.
Preferred phone: uses Phone first via COALESCE; falls back to MobilePhone if Phone is null.
Account name looked up by joining Contact.AccountId = Account.Id (LEFT JOIN so persons without an account still appear).
Salesforce OwnerId is a text Id like '005NS00000y00YZYAY'; extracts the trailing numeric suffix with SUBSTRING and casts to integer as a Pipedrive owner placeholder.
Contact creation timestamp, preserved directly from CreatedDate.

tables.reasoning: The model joins the raw contact table with the raw account table on AccountId to resolve organization names. Contact.Id is used as sf_contact_id for traceability, while FirstName and LastName are concatenated into a clean full name. Email is normalized to lowercase+trimmed. Phone prioritizes the primary phone field over mobile. OwnerId is coerced to integer by extracting its trailing numeric portion using PostgreSQL's regex SUBSTRING syntax, with NULL preserved where owner_id is absent.

column_notes.explanation: Directly mapped from the source Account ID to preserve Salesforce traceability and enable idempotent downstream loads.
Account name trimmed to remove leading/trailing whitespace as required by the Pipedrive-style output schema.
Billing address components concatenated with commas; CONCAT_WS is used to safely skip NULL fields without introducing extra separators.
Owner ID coerced to INTEGER where it contains only numeric characters, otherwise set to NULL as specified.
Mapped directly from the source Account creation timestamp to track record ingestion/add time.

tables.reasoning: The model performs a direct transformation of the Salesforce Account table into the target organizations schema. It applies string trimming for normalization, leverages CONCAT_WS for robust multi-field address formatting that gracefully handles missing values, and coerces the owner identifier to an integer where possible per the specification.

column_notes.explanation: Preserved directly from the Salesforce Contact ID, cast to text.
Combined First Name and Last Name using CONCAT_WS to handle nulls cleanly, with trimming applied.
Extracted from the Email field, lowercased and trimmed to standard format.
Falls back to MobilePhone if Phone is missing, with whitespace trimmed.
Retrieved by joining Contact.AccountId to Account.Id. Trimmed for cleanliness.
Attempted coercion from text to integer using regex validation; returns null if not a valid integer string.
Mapped from the Contact CreatedDate timestamp.

tables.reasoning: I joined the raw `contact` table to the `account` table using a left join on the standard Salesforce ID relationship (`contact.account_id = account.id`) to fetch the organization name. The logic handles data quality by trimming strings, lowercasing emails, and using `CONCAT_WS` for robust name assembly. For the integer mapping of `owner_id`, I utilized a standard regex check (`~ '^\d+$'`) to safely coerce values to integers without failing on non-numeric strings (like Salesforce User IDs).

column_notes.explanation: Mapped from source `id`, cast to text for consistent traceability and idempotent loading.
Trimmed leading/trailing whitespace and cast to text to match target schema constraints.
Concatenated billing location fields separated by commas, trimming trailing separators to prevent dangling punctuation.
Casted from varchar to integer placeholder using NULLIF to safely handle empty strings or non-numeric source values.
Source creation timestamp cast to standard timestamp type for the Pipedrive-style schema.

tables.reasoning: Directly maps Salesforce Account fields to the target Pipedrive-style organizations schema. Handles nulls in address components gracefully by concatenating with commas and trimming edges. Safely coerces the varchar OwnerId to an integer placeholder and casts timestamps appropriately for the target types.

tables.reasoning: I mapped the Salesforce Account columns directly to the target schema, applying `trim()` for the name and `concat_ws()` with individual trims for the address summary. tables.description: Pipedrive-style organization candidates from Salesforce Account.

columns.name: sf_account_id
columns.description: Salesforce Account Id (preserve for traceability).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Account Name; trimmed.

columns.name: address
columns.description: Billing address summary — concatenate BillingStreet, BillingCity, BillingState, BillingPostalCode, BillingCountry with commas/spaces.

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map OwnerId when possible.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Account.CreatedDate if present.

tables.name: persons
tables.description: Pipedrive-style person candidates from Salesforce Contact. Join account on AccountId for organization name when needed.

columns.name: sf_contact_id
columns.description: Salesforce Contact Id (preserve for traceability and idempotent load later).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full display name — trim; combine FirstName and LastName with a single space.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Primary email; lowercased and trimmed (use Email if present).

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Preferred phone — Phone or MobilePhone.

columns.name: org_name
columns.description: Account name looked up via Contact.AccountId = Account.Id (staging table account).

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map from OwnerId if you can coerce to integer, otherwise null.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Contact.CreatedDate if available.
